edible_parts: Leaves (roasted → North America's only native caffeine tea); berries TOXIC.
other_uses: Evergreen holly of the Southeast whose leaves brew the caffeinated 'black drink' (cassina).
prep: Dry and roast the leaves for 'yaupon tea' — the only caffeine-bearing plant native to North America (despite the alarming Latin name, it does not cause vomiting in normal tea). The red berries, however, are toxic and NOT eaten.
soil: Moist to dry, well-drained soils; very adaptable.
risks: Berries toxic — leaves only, for tea.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
